title: The Illusion Of War
content:
War
        I abhor,
        And yet how sweet
        The sound along the marching street
        Of drum and fife, and I forget
        Wet eyes of widows, and forget
        Broken old mothers, and the whole
        Dark butchery without a soul.

        Without a soul - save this bright drink
        Of heady music, sweet as hell;
        And even my peace-abiding feet
        Go marching with the marching street,
        For yonder, yonder goes the fife,
        And what care I for human life!
        The tears fill my astonished eyes
        And my full heart is like to break,
        And yet 'tis all embannered lies,
        A dream those little drummers make.

        O it is wickedness to clothe
        Yon hideous grinning thing that stalks
        Hidden in music, like a queen
        That in a garden of glory walks,
        Till good men love the thing they loathe.
        Art, thou hast many infamies,
        But not an infamy like this;
        O snap the fife and still the drum,
        And show the monster as she is.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Richard Le Gallienne delivers a powerful performance in "The Illusion Of War"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
